message:
chore(beads): close beads_rust-6ck2 (already fixed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -278,7 +278,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-69p","title":"JSONL Import Implementation","description":"# JSONL Import Implementation

## Purpose
Implement classic JSONL import semantics with collision detection, prefix validation, tombstone protection, and depth-ordered creation.

## Pipeline (classic)
1. **Conflict marker scan** (`<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>`) → abort.
2. Parse JSONL with 2MB buffer.
3. Normalize:
   - Recompute content_hash (ignore JSONL value)
   - If ID contains `-wisp-`, set `ephemeral=true`
   - Apply defaults (`SetDefaults`) + closed_at invariant repair
4. **Clear export_hashes** before import.
5. Prefix validation (skip in multi-repo or `SkipPrefixValidation=true`).
6. Collision detection (4-phase): external_ref → content_hash → ID → new.
7. Tombstone protection: if DB has tombstone for ID, **skip** incoming.
8. Orphan handling: `strict|resurrect|skip|allow`.
9. Create issues depth-by-depth (parents first).
10. Sync deps/labels/comments after issues.
11. Refresh blocked cache.
12. Update metadata (`jsonl_content_hash`, `last_import_time`).

## Prefix Mismatch
- If mismatches are only tombstones, drop silently.
- `--rename-on-import` rewrites IDs **and** text references in title/desc/design/acceptance/notes/comments/deps.
- Otherwise error.

## External Ref Duplicates
- Default: error on duplicate external_ref.
- `--clear-duplicate-external-refs`: keep first, clear others.

## Output
- Legacy import does **not** emit structured JSON summary; output is stderr text.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Collision handling matches bd (timestamp-gated updates; equal timestamps skip).
- Tombstones never resurrect.
- Export hashes cleared before import.

## Tests
- Prefix mismatch + rename-on-import.
- Tombstone protection.
- Orphan handling modes.
- Collision detection order.","notes":"JSONL import fully implemented in src/sync/mod.rs: import_from_jsonl() implements complete pipeline - conflict marker scanning, 2MB buffered JSONL parsing, normalization, prefix validation, 4-phase collision detection, tombstone protection, external ref duplicate handling, issue upsert, relation sync (deps/labels/comments), blocked cache rebuild, metadata update. All storage methods implemented in sqlite.rs. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"GraySparrow","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:32:20.703816108Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:34:29.666558420Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:34:29.666558420Z","close_reason":"JSONL import fully verified - all 3 acceptance criteria met: timestamp-gated updates with equal timestamp skip, tombstone protection never resurrects, export hashes cleared before import. All tests pass (399 lib + integration).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-6azu","title":"Regenerate CLI reference from current Clap definitions","description":"Reality-check finding: docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md has drifted from src/cli/mod.rs in multiple places: config is documented as flag-style options even though it is subcommand-based, delete documents -f/--force as skip confirmation while code uses --force to orphan dependents and also has --cascade/--hard/--dry-run/--from-file, query save is documented as save <NAME> <QUERY> while code saves flattened list filters, and sync omits several current flags. Acceptance: regenerate or manually reconcile CLI_REFERENCE against Clap output and add a doc check to prevent command-reference drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:06:35.829067894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:36:00.496527405Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:36:00.496264773Z","close_reason":"Reconciled CLI_REFERENCE with current Clap surfaces for config, delete, query, sync and adjacent commands; added a Clap-backed unit guard that requires all top-level command headings plus high-risk drift sentinels to remain documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","docs","reality-check"],"comments":[{"id":283,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6azu","author":"ubuntu","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail health is red; intended files are docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md plus focused documentation drift test/check files after inspection","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:24:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-6bs2","title":"Neutralize terminal control sequences in rendered issue fields","description":"Lens: Adversary

Finding: User-controlled issue fields can contain terminal control sequences. Titles, descriptions, comments, authors, and some event text are accepted with length-only validation and are rendered by multiple human-output paths. A malicious local contributor could craft issue data that clears the terminal, rewrites previous lines, spoofs output, or triggers terminal-specific OSC behavior when another operator runs `br list`, `br show`, `br audit`, or related commands.

Evidence:
- `src/validation/mod.rs` validates title/comment emptiness and length but not C0/C1/ANSI/OSC control characters.
- Several CLI and formatter paths print issue titles/comments/event text directly for human output.
- JSON output should remain raw, but human terminal output needs defensive rendering.

Acceptance criteria:
- Human output visibly escapes or strips dangerous terminal controls in all user-controlled text fields.
- JSON/robot output preserves raw values for machine consumers.
- Regression tests cover ANSI clear-screen, carriage return/backspace spoofing, and OSC-style sequences in titles/comments.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:06:23.564484722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:28:46.826462035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:28:46.826196277Z","close_reason":"Escaped terminal control characters in human output while preserving raw JSON; added sanitizer unit tests and E2E coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adversary","audit","output","terminal"]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-6ck2","title":"[P2] MCP mutations swallow auto-flush and sync-lock failures","description":"Root cause: src/mcp/mod.rs:69-76 stores try_sync_lock as an Option and never checks whether lock acquisition or lock-file open succeeded; even if it is None, with_mutation still calls auto_flush. Any auto_flush error is logged at debug as non-fatal and the mutating MCP tool returns success. src/sync/mod.rs:143-151 also collapses lock-file open errors into None, the same shape as lock contention.

Repro: run br serve, make .beads/issues.jsonl unwritable or leave conflict markers/permission failure, then call MCP create_issue/update_issue. SQLite mutation can commit and the MCP client receives success while JSONL remains stale or the export ran without a sync lock.

Proposed fix sketch: make try_sync_lock return Result<Option<File>>. In MCP with_mutation, do not run auto_flush without a sync lock, and surface auto_flush/export failure as a structured MCP error or explicit warning while preserving dirty metadata. Keep successful mutation state auditable.

Test to add: MCP mutation with an unwritable/conflicted JSONL asserts the tool surfaces the flush failure and dirty state remains; held .sync.lock asserts no unguarded export occurs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"agent_2282484","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:10.372672289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:43:56.921372951Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":336,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ck2","author":"beads_rust__p7","text":"degraded-coordination: files: src/mcp/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, tests/mcp_*.rs or inline MCP tests as needed","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:33:47Z"},{"id":337,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ck2","author":"beads_rust__p7","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable in this session; files: src/mcp/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, tests/mcp_auto_flush.rs if a focused integration test is needed","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:32Z"},{"id":338,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ck2","author":"beads_rust__p7","text":"release: accidental duplicate claim from this pane; no files touched, leaving bead open for another agent","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:36Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-6ck2","title":"[P2] MCP mutations swallow auto-flush and sync-lock failures","description":"Root cause: src/mcp/mod.rs:69-76 stores try_sync_lock as an Option and never checks whether lock acquisition or lock-file open succeeded; even if it is None, with_mutation still calls auto_flush. Any auto_flush error is logged at debug as non-fatal and the mutating MCP tool returns success. src/sync/mod.rs:143-151 also collapses lock-file open errors into None, the same shape as lock contention.

Repro: run br serve, make .beads/issues.jsonl unwritable or leave conflict markers/permission failure, then call MCP create_issue/update_issue. SQLite mutation can commit and the MCP client receives success while JSONL remains stale or the export ran without a sync lock.

Proposed fix sketch: make try_sync_lock return Result<Option<File>>. In MCP with_mutation, do not run auto_flush without a sync lock, and surface auto_flush/export failure as a structured MCP error or explicit warning while preserving dirty metadata. Keep successful mutation state auditable.

Test to add: MCP mutation with an unwritable/conflicted JSONL asserts the tool surfaces the flush failure and dirty state remains; held .sync.lock asserts no unguarded export occurs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"agent_2282484","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:10.372672289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:44:52.349889987Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:44:52.349477605Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current code. try_sync_lock returns Result<Option<File>> (errors propagate, not collapsed to None). with_mutation refuses mutation without sync lock and surfaces auto_flush failures as structured MCP errors. Existing tests verify both paths (with_mutation_requires_openable_sync_lock_before_mutating, with_mutation_reports_auto_flush_failure_and_preserves_dirty_state). No code change needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":336,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ck2","author":"beads_rust__p7","text":"degraded-coordination: files: src/mcp/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, tests/mcp_*.rs or inline MCP tests as needed","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:33:47Z"},{"id":337,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ck2","author":"beads_rust__p7","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable in this session; files: src/mcp/mod.rs, src/sync/mod.rs, tests/mcp_auto_flush.rs if a focused integration test is needed","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:32Z"},{"id":338,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ck2","author":"beads_rust__p7","text":"release: accidental duplicate claim from this pane; no files touched, leaving bead open for another agent","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-6d9t","title":"[LOW] hex_encode lacks exhaustive byte-pattern fixtures","description":"Root cause: src/util/hash.rs:13 is the new sha2 0.11 hex formatter path used for digest output. Current inline tests at src/util/hash.rs:299 cover empty, single bytes, 0..31, one high-bit sample, lowercase, and hello-world SHA256, but they do not verify digest-width all-zero, digest-width all-0xff, all 256 byte values, or arbitrary non-UTF8/embedded-NUL byte vectors against an independent reference formatter. Repro: a regression that drops leading zero nibbles for [0; 32], mishandles repeated 0xff, or corrupts high-bit/non-UTF8 bytes could still pass the current samples. Proposed fix: add inline tests or a small proptest comparing hex_encode(bytes) to bytes.iter().map(|b| format!("{b:02x}")).collect::<String>() for arbitrary Vec<u8>, plus explicit [0;32], [0xff;32], and 0..=255 fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:29.495344118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:50:45.183257281Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:50:45.182815144Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af38921 + 16ccd2c: proptest roundtrip with arbitrary bytes, plus explicit all-zero, all-ff, and all-256-values fixtures. Full coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-6esx","title":"E2E scenarios: workspace init + config/doctor/info/where/version","description":"Implement E2E scenarios for core workspace commands and diagnostics.

Coverage
- init (new workspace, re-init with --force handling)
- config get/set/list/edit (validate precedence)
- doctor (read-only diagnostics)
- info + where (paths + metadata)
- version (json + text)

Acceptance
- Uses harness + dataset registry; logs full artifacts.
- Assertions cover exit codes, JSON shapes, and filesystem effects.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleLake","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:41:05.936532728Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-20T21:02:09.835570407Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T21:02:09.835521354Z","close_reason":"E2E scenarios complete: All 140+ tests pass for workspace commands (init, config, doctor, info, where, version). Coverage includes JSON shapes, exit codes, filesystem effects. Uses harness + dataset registry. All acceptance criteria met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-7wqg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-9ks6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-enep","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-hdc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-o1az","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-pnvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-r23m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rkuz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-x7z8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":86,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Opus-45-Claude fixed 3 failing tests in e2e_workspace_commands.rs:
1. e2e_info_json_output: Added database_path to checked fields (br uses this field)
2. e2e_init_already_initialized: Accepts ALREADY_INITIALIZED error code in stderr
3. e2e_config_get_set: Changed to use unique test key (test_custom_key) to avoid DB default conflicts

Also fixed a config.rs bug that was causing config set to fail: YAML files with only comments were parsed as Null, causing the config to be overwritten with 'null'.

All 136 tests in e2e_workspace_commands.rs now pass. Changes committed in 8836bd6.","created_at":"2026-01-18T06:52:37Z"},{"id":87,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coordinator observation (Opus-45-Claude):

Found failing tests in e2e_workspace_scenarios:
- scenario_init_reinit_is_idempotent
- scenario_init_json_output  
- scenario_workspace_lifecycle

Root cause: Tests expect `init` to be idempotent, but both br and bd reject re-init:
- br returns: ALREADY_INITIALIZED error (exit 2)
- bd returns: 'This workspace is already initialized', 'Aborting'

Fix options:
1. Update tests to NOT expect idempotent init (align with actual br/bd behavior)
2. Tests should use new temp workspace for each scenario

Test run: 129 passed, 3 failed","created_at":"2026-01-18T07:25:19Z"},{"id":88,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Updated e2e_workspace_scenarios: re-init now expects ALREADY_INITIALIZED failure (matches br/bd); init --json test now accepts non-JSON output; lifecycle scenario updated accordingly.","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:50:12Z"},{"id":89,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added E2E coverage for config edit (EDITOR=true) in tests/e2e_workspace_commands.rs; also fixed unused variable warning in repro_collision_labels.rs while running required checks.","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:02:33Z"},{"id":90,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6esx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WildStream: ran cargo test --test e2e_workspace_scenarios and --test e2e_workspace_commands on 2026-01-18; both passed locally (132/132 and 137/137).  {"id":"beads_rust-6ii1","title":"Fix 61 pre-existing test failures caused by fsqlite API issues","description":"58 out of 783 tests fail (was 61, fixed 3 via last_insert_rowid). Root causes: (1) OpenRead cursor failures on root pages >2 [21 tests] - B-tree cursor limitation; (2) prepare() SELECT-only restriction [11 tests]; (3) Data count mismatches after INSERT [11 tests]; (4) Schema migration failures [5 tests]; (5) Bind parameter index out of range [2 tests]; (6) ORDER BY validation [1 test]; (7) Datetime parsing [2 tests]; (8) PoisonError cascades [2 tests]; (9) Test isolation [1 test]; (10) Schema validation on root page 2 [1 test]. Primary blockers are fsqlite B-tree multi-table support and DML prepared statements.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:40:18.491852494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T03:50:07.838820057Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T03:50:07.838801463Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":93,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ii1","author":"LavenderSummit","text":"Progress: Fixed last_insert_rowid() in fsqlite (3 tests fixed, 61->58). Remaining 58 failures categorized into 10 root causes - see description update.","created_at":"2026-02-20T21:18:49Z"},{"id":94,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ii1","author":"LavenderSummit","text":"Progress: 61→45 failures. Fixed prepare() for INSERT statements by replacing conn.prepare() with conn.execute_with_params() in rebuild_blocked_cache_impl and set_export_hashes. Also fixed last_insert_rowid() via fsqlite-func thread-locals. 13 more tests passing. Remaining 45 are deeper fsqlite issues: OpenRead cursor on higher root pages (21), data visibility/count mismatches (11), schema migration (5), bind params (2), ORDER BY validation (1), datetime parsing (2), init isolation (1), import update (1), unique constraint (1).","created_at":"2026-02-20T21:28:23Z"},{"id":95,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ii1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: 61→40 failures (21 fixed). Fixed: last_insert_rowid threading (3), prepare SELECT-only (13), UNIQUE constraint (3), EXISTS bind params (2). Remaining 40 are deep fsqlite B-tree/pager issues.","created_at":"2026-02-20T22:40:49Z"},{"id":96,"issue_id":"beads_rust-6ii1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 61 test failures resolved (785 pass, 0 fail). Fixed: BinaryOp::EqEq, ORDER BY, CTE->BFS, PRAGMA table_info, CHECK constraint.","created_at":"2026-02-21T03:50:03Z"}]}
